title: practical english usage - Part 306
content:
‘ Your mother's upset with you.' ‘ I couldn't care less.' (= ɪ  don’ t care at all.) ‘ I'll never speak to you again.' ‘ I don't care.' I don't care whether it rains - I'm happy.

(     . . .

Most people care about other people's opinions.

is usually left out before a conjunction.

This is very common in negative sentences. About is used before an object, but Care (about) is used to say whether you feel something is important to you.

‘ Bye, Ruth.' ‘ Bye, Mike. Take care.' Take care when you're crossing the road, children.

formula when saying goodbye.

Take care (without a preposition) means 'be careful’ . Some people use it as a Ms Savage takes care of marketing and I'm responsible for production.

It's no good giving Daniel a rabbit: he's too young to take care of it.

Nurses take care of sick people.

Take care of normally means 'look after’  or 'take responsibility for’ .

She's a selfish woman, but somehow you can't help liking her.

in British English), you mean that you can't stop yourself, even if you don't If you say that you cannot!can't help doing something (especially 418  can't     help      _       _ ____________ Word  Problems from A  to Z      Section 31 vocabulary •  420  city and town not                                                                  not a closed door (     a-shut-deor) closed eyes (     shut eyes) Shut is not usually used before a noun.

The office is closed/shut on Saturday afternoon.

The  past participles closed and shut can be used as adjectives.

2 past    participles She shut him  out of the house.

I shut the papers in my desk drawer and locked it. (     /  dosed the papers ...) You can shut, but not close, somebody/something in or out of a place.

The shop closes/shuts at five o'clock.

I can 't close/shut the window. Can you help me?

Open your mouth  and close/shut your eyes.

Close /kləuz/  and shut can often be used with the same meaning.

422 close      and   shut It's hard to learn classical guitar.

Szymanowski, in contrast to popular music or jazz.

Classical music is used to refer to the work of composers like Bach, Mozart or She's studying classical languages and literature at Cambridge.

of art of the so-called ’ classical’  period in the 18th century.

Classical refers to the culture of ancient Greece and Rome, or to European works She buys classic cars and restores them.

He's a classic 1960s hippy who has never changed.

Classic usually refers to a famous traditional style.

with city often preferred in more prestigious contexts. Compare: The  two words can be used more or less interchangeably in American English, I'm not sure what his job is: something in The City.

The City is also a name for London’ s financial centre.
